channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: SalesSim: Benchmarking and Aligning Multimodal Language Models as Retail User Simulators
item.description: arXiv:2605.08334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present SalesSim, a framework and testbed for evaluating the ability of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to simulate realistic, persona-driven customer behavior in multi-turn, multi-modal, tool-augmented online retail conversations. Unlike ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, experiment, augmented, RAG, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sanity Checks for Long-Form Hallucination Detection
item.description: arXiv:2605.08346v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hallucination detection methods for large language models increasingly operate on chain-of-thought reasoning traces, yet it remains unclear whether they evaluate the reasoning itself or merely exploit surface correlates of the final answer. We introdu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, announcement, large language model, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Change My View? The Dynamics of Persuasion and Polarization in Online Discourse
item.description: arXiv:2605.08383v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Philosophical accounts of persuasion often assume that shared evidence and rational argumentation should lead to a convergence of views between peers, yet everyday discourse often suggests otherwise. In this study, we use large language models to anal...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, study, large language model, model, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: jina-embeddings-v5-omni: Text-Geometry-Preserving Multimodal Embeddings via Frozen-Tower Composition
item.description: arXiv:2605.08384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this work, we introduce frozen-encoder model composition, a novel approach to multimodal embedding models. We build on the VLM-style architecture, in which non-text encoders are adapted to produce input for a language model, which in turn generates...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, multimodal, arxiv, audio, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AIPO: : Learning to Reason from Active Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2605.08401v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable reasoning capabilities, largely stimulated by Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). However, existing RL algorithms face a fundamental limitation: their expl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, experiment, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Built Environment Reasoning from Remote Sensing Imagery Using Large Vision--Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.08404v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work investigates the use of large language models (LLMs) for tasks in smart cities. The core idea is to leverage remote sensing imagery to characterize the built environment, including design suggestions, constructability assessment, landuse pat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, RAG, large language model, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Semantic-Sampling Framework for Evaluating Calibration in Open-Ended Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2605.08432v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Calibration measures whether a model's predicted confidence aligns with its empirical accuracy, and is central to the reliable deployment of large language models (LLMs) in high-stakes domains such as medicine and law. While much recent work focuses o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, LLM, experiment, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Magis-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Magistrate-Level Legal Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2605.08437v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing benchmarks for legal AI focus primarily on tasks where LLMs must produce legal arguments or documents, yet the capacity to \emph{judge} such arguments -- weighing competing claims, applying doctrine to facts, and rendering reasoned decisions ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, RAG, release, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Where Reliability Lives in Vision-Language Models: A Mechanistic Study of Attention, Hidden States, and Causal Circuits
item.description: arXiv:2605.08200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A pervasive intuition holds that vision-language models (VLMs) are most trustworthy when their attention maps look sharp: concentrated attention on the queried region should imply a confident, calibrated answer. We test this Attention-Confidence Assum...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, RAG, attention, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spatial Priming Outperforms Semantic Prompting: A Grid-Based Approach to Improving LLM Accuracy on Chart Data Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2605.08220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The automated extraction of data from scientific charts is a critical task for large-scale literature analysis. While multimodal Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise, their accuracy on non-standardized charts remains a challenge. This raises a ke...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, LLM, experiment, prompting, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Auto-Rubric as Reward: From Implicit Preferences to Explicit Multimodal Generative Criteria
item.description: arXiv:2605.08354v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Aligning multimodal generative models with human preferences demands reward signals that respect the compositional, multi-dimensional structure of human judgment. Prevailing RLHF approaches reduce this structure to scalar or pairwise labels, collapsin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> few-shot, RLHF, image, multimodal, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Embeddings for Preferences, Not Semantics
item.description: arXiv:2605.08360v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern AI is opening the door to collective decision-making in which participants express their views as free-form text rather than voting on a fixed set of candidates. A natural idea is to embed these opinions in a vector space so that the substantia...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, arxiv, embedding, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On Distinguishing Capability Elicitation from Capability Creation in Post-Training: A Free-Energy Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2605.08368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Debates about large language model post-training often treat supervised fine-tuning (SFT) as imitation and reinforcement learning (RL) as discovery. But this distinction is too coarse. What matters is whether a training procedure increases the probabi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, large language model, model, arxiv, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MemQ: Integrating Q-Learning into Self-Evolving Memory Agents over Provenance DAGs
item.description: arXiv:2605.08374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Episodic memory allows LLM agents to accumulate and retrieve experience, but current methods treat each memory independently, i.e., evaluating retrieval quality in isolation without accounting for the dependency chains through which memories enable th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, study, LLM, memory, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SkillLens: Adaptive Multi-Granularity Skill Reuse for Cost-Efficient LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.08386v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Skill libraries have become a practical way for LLM agents to reuse procedural experience across tasks. However, existing systems typically treat skills as flat, single-resolution prompt blocks. This creates a tension between relevance and cost: injec...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, analysis, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CoCoDA: Co-evolving Compositional DAG for Tool-Augmented Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.08399v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-augmented language models can extend small language models with external executable skills, but scaling the tool library creates a coupled challenge: the library must evolve with the planner as new reusable subroutines emerge, while retrieval fro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, augmented, retrieval, context, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Belief or Circuitry? Causal Evidence for In-Context Graph Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.08405v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How do LLMs learn in-context? Is it by pattern-matching recent tokens, or by inferring latent structure? We probe this question using a toy graph random-walk across two competing graph structures. This task's answer is, in principle, decidable: either...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, in-context, context, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BaLoRA: Bayesian Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Scale Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.08110v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) has become the standard for fine-tuning large pre-trained models at reduced computational cost. However, its low-rank point-estimate updates limit expressiveness, leave a persistent gap relative to full fine-tuning accuracy,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, fine-tuning, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TTCD:Transformer Integrated Temporal Causal Discovery from Non-Stationary Time Series Data
item.description: arXiv:2605.08111v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The widespread availability of complex time series data in various domains such as environmental science, epidemiology, and economics demands robust causal discovery methods that can identify intricate contemporaneous and lagged relationships in non-s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, experiment, context, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt engineering, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, RAG, retrieval, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: Effective Explanations Support Planning Under Uncertainty
item.description: arXiv:2605.08406v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Explaining how to get from A to B can be challenging. It requires mentally simulating what the listener will do based on what they are told. To capture this process, we propose a computational model that converts utterances into action plans: a large ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Do Foundation Model Embeddings Improve Cross-Country Crop Yield Generalisation? A Leave-One-Country-Out Evaluation in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.description: arXiv:2605.08113v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate predictions of smallholder maize yields across national boundaries are critical for food security planning in sub-Saharan Africa, yet most published benchmarks report within-country performance that overstates true generalisability. This pape...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, paper, arxiv, model, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: How Much Do Circuits Tell Us? Measuring the Consistency and Specificity of Language Model Circuits
item.description: arXiv:2605.08348v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The circuits framework in mechanistic interpretability aims to identify causally important sparse subgraphs of model components, typically evaluated by measuring necessity and sufficiency. We measure circuit reuse, the proportion of components shared ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, model, arxiv, example, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Path-Based Gradient Boosting for Graph-Level Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.08102v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose PathBoost, a gradient tree boosting method for graph-level classification and regression that learns discriminative path-based features directly from the input graph structure. Building on a previous work, which was tailored to a specific c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Distributional Reinforcement Learning via the Cram\'er Distance
item.description: arXiv:2605.08104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper explores the application of the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm within a Distributional Reinforcement Learning setting and introduces an implementation of such algorithm named Cram\'er-based Distributional Soft Actor-Critic (C-DSAC). The ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, paper, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, example, tool, prompt engineering, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Reinforcement learning for inverse structural design and rapid laser cutting of kirigami prototypes
item.description: arXiv:2605.08098v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Kirigami is an increasingly useful fabrication method to produce shape-programmable metamaterial structures. However, inverse design remains difficult because deployment is nonlinear, and feasible cut layouts must satisfy discrete compatibility rules,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Geometry-free prediction of inertial lift forces in microfluidic devices using deep learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.08109v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inertial microfluidic devices (IMDs) offer low-cost, high-throughput alternative techniques for many traditional particle- (or cell-) manipulation tasks, but simulating them requires being able to predict particle migration, and thus particle lift for...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, ICL, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Safety-Aware Denoiser for Text Diffusion Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.08116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work on text diffusion models offers a promising alternative to autoregressive generation, but controlling their safety remains underexplored. Existing safety approaches are geared toward autoregressive models and typically rely on post-hoc fil...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Feature Repulsion and Spectral Lock-in: An Empirical Study of Two-Layer Network Grokking
item.description: arXiv:2605.08119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tian (2025) proves a repulsion theorem (Theorem 6) for the matrix $ B = (\widetilde{F}^\top \widetilde{F} + \eta I)^{-1} $ during the interactive feature-learning stage of grokking: similar features have negative off-diagonal entries $ B_{j\ell} $, pr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
